prs.title: docs(readme): fix badge formatting
prs.body:
## Description                                                                                                                                                                                    
                  
  Fixes README badge formatting by consolidating badges that were incorrectly split across multiple lines into single lines. This ensures proper rendering across all Markdown parsers.             
   
  ## Changes                                                                                                                                                                                        
                  
  | Badge | Fixed |
  |-------|-------|
  | Package Manager CI | Split lines → Single line |
  | Web site | Split lines → Single line |                                                                                                                                                          
  | NPM version | Split lines → Single line |                                                                                                                                                       
  | NPM downloads | Split lines → Single line |                                                                                                                                                     
  | Security Responsible Disclosure | Split lines → Single line |

prs.title: fix: allow hyphens and spaces in theme generator theme name
prs.body:
Fixes #4387.
The theme generator silently discards all changes when the theme name contains a hyphen (e.g. my-theme) or a space (e.g. cringe classic). This is caused by an overly restrictive regex in `themeGeneratorValidation.js `that only accepts `[a-z]` characters.

## changes made
- Updated themeNamePattern in `themeGeneratorValidation.js` to allow lowercase letters, digits, spaces, and hyphens (while continuing to prevent them at the very start or end of the name).
- Added `.trim()` to the validation pipeline so that trailing spaces typed by users don't temporarily break the validator or pollute the generated output.



https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6de1f64d-a0cb-4aab-a308-e32e084ee907

This ensures full compliance with the strict SVGLint coordinate rules before formatting it with SVGO.

prs.title: feat: add context.reportAppFailure() for failure notifications (#1934)
prs.body:
## Description
This PR addresses issue #1934 which requested a native method for Probot apps to alert users of execution failures (similar to how Dependabot generates alerts). 

Since GitHub apps do not have a specific "failure alert" API, the most robust native integration is to create a GitHub Issue in the context repository. This PR adds a `reportAppFailure` helper to the `Context` object, giving developers a one-line method to report crashes.

### Changes made
- **`src/context.ts`**: Added `context.reportAppFailure()`. This generates a pre-formatted GitHub issue on the repository containing:
  - The Event name
  - The Webhook delivery ID (for debugging)
  - The Error stack trace or message
  - A `"probot-app-failure"` label
- **`test/context.test.ts`**: Added comprehensive unit tests simulating octokit's `issues.create` method to verify behavior and ensure backward compatibility.
- **`docs/best-practices.md`**: Added a new "Error Reporting" section documenting how developers can use the feature to gracefully handle and alert repo owners of workflow crashes.

## Verification
- Ran existing unit tests (`npm test`); all 200 pass.
- Visually verified output using local Probot script to trigger issues against a live repository.

Fixes #1934

prs.title: calc: add Shuffle command to NotebookBar and Menubar
prs.body:
* Resolves: #14986
* Target version: main

### Summary

New command Shuffle (`Data -> Shuffle`) was added to CO Classic (26.04) but was not available in COOL since it was not yet added to the NotebookBar.

This PR adds the Shuffle command to:
- The **Data** tab in the NotebookBar (as a toolitem in the Sort group, alongside Sort Ascending/Descending)
- The **Data** menu in the classic Menubar (both desktop and mobile spreadsheet views)
- The `unocommands.js` translation registry

Shuffle works similarly to Sort but randomly shuffles elements instead of ordering them, which is useful for statistics.

### Checklist

- [x] All commits have Change-Id
- [x] Documentation (manuals or wiki) has been updated or is not required

prs.title: Fix EXIF profile picture orientation bug (#34275)

labels.name: 4: Reviews Complete
labels.description: All reviewers have approved the pull request

labels.name: Changelog/Done
labels.description: Required changelog entry has been written

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

labels.name: release-note
labels.description: Denotes a PR that will be considered when it comes time to generate release notes.

prs.body:
Fixes #34275.

**Issue:**
When a user uploads a profile picture containing EXIF orientation metadata (e.g. from an iOS device), the image is rotated incorrectly upon saving. 

**Root Cause:**
In `AdjustImage`, the file reader was consumed fully by `imgDecoder.Decode(file)`. Immediately after, `imaging.GetImageOrientation` was called on the same exhausted reader, which silently failed and defaulted to returning an `Upright` orientation, preventing `MakeImageUpright` from correcting the rotation.

**Fix:**
Added a `file.Seek(0, io.SeekStart)` call immediately before `GetImageOrientation` is invoked, to ensure the EXIF metadata can be successfully read.

**Verification:**
Added a sub-test in `TestAdjustProfileImage` that utilizes an implicitly rotated JPEG from `tests/exif_samples/right.jpg` and verifies the final profile image correctly adjusts into an upright 128x128 square.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Change Impact: 🟡 Medium

**Reasoning:** The fix is a small, targeted change in profile image processing that restores EXIF orientation handling by seeking the reader back to the start before reading metadata; it affects a user-visible image handling path used across upload flows. Tests were added to cover EXIF orientation scenarios, reducing uncertainty.

**Regression Risk:** Low — the change is limited to a single function in image adjustment and only adds a seek with defensive logging on failure; it does not modify public APIs or persistent data and has unit test coverage for the reported EXIF case.

**QA Recommendation:** Manually verify profile picture uploads from devices that embed EXIF orientation (iOS/Android) across primary upload paths (web UI, imports, plugin API) for 90°, 180°, and 270° rotations. Automated tests cover the added EXIF sample cases, but perform quick visual checks in staging to ensure no regressions in other image formats.
*Generated by CodeRabbitAI*
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

```release-note
Fixed a bug that caused profile pictures to be incorrectly rotated on upload, as their EXIF rotation tag was ignored.
```

prs.title: fix(tooltip): show payloads when chart has multiple X axes. Fixes #4757
prs.body:
## Description
This PR fixes an issue where the `Tooltip` fails to display payloads for all graphical items when a chart utilizes multiple axes (e.g., dual X-axes).

The fix involves two key changes:

- **Selection Logic**: Updated `tooltipSelectors.ts` to ensure all graphical items are considered for the tooltip payload regardless of their axis ID. Previously, items on secondary axes were filtered out during the "active" axis detection phase.
- **Lookup Fallback**: Updated `combineTooltipPayload.ts `to include a fallback to index-based lookup. When a chart has multiple axes with different `dataKey` names, the standard data-key-based lookup fails for the secondary axis items. The fallback ensures these items can still find and contribute their data to the tooltip via their index in the data array.
<!--- Describe your changes in detail -->

## Related Issue
Fixes #4757
<!--- This project only accepts pull requests related to open issues -->
<!--- If suggesting a new feature or change, please discuss it in an issue first -->
<!--- If fixing a bug, there should be an issue describing it with steps to reproduce -->
<!--- Please link to the issue here: -->

## Motivation and Context
Previously, the Tooltip was implicitly restricted to items on the "active" tooltip axis. This caused items attached to a secondary` <XAxis />` or` <YAxis />` to be either completely missing from the tooltip or only show up if they happened to share the same `dataKey` as the primary axis. This fix ensures that the Tooltip behaves as a consolidated view for all chart data, regardless of axis layout.
<!--- Why is this change required? What problem does it solve? -->

## How Has This Been Tested?
- New Test: `npx vitest run test/component/Tooltip/Tooltip.multipleXAxis.spec.tsx` (Passed)
- Library Tests: `npx vitest run test/state/selectors/selectors.spec.tsx` (Passed)
- Full Suite: `npm run test` (All 14,137 tests passed).

<!--- Please describe in detail how you tested your changes. -->
<!--- Include details of your testing environment, and the tests you ran to -->
<!--- see how your change affects other areas of the code, etc. -->

## Screenshots (if appropriate):

## Types of changes

<!--- What types of changes does your code introduce? Put an `x` in all the boxes that apply: -->

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)

## Checklist:

<!--- Go over all the following points, and put an `x` in all the boxes that apply. -->
<!--- If you're unsure about any of these, don't hesitate to ask. We're here to help! -->

- [ ] My change requires a change to the documentation.
- [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests to cover my changes.
- [x] I have added a storybook story or VR test, or extended an existing story or VR test to show my changes


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Tooltips now reliably include series from secondary or multiple X axes and handle differing data key arrangements, preserving tooltip items instead of dropping them.
  * Tooltip matching/synchronization across axes improved so hovered points consistently show the expected combined payload.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests validating tooltip behavior and payload consistency for charts with multiple X axes.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Fix: Add troubleshooting steps for submodule setup (SSH → HTTPS)
prs.body:
## Description
When i setting up the repository,  i encounter the following error during submodule initialization:
```
git@github.com: Permission denied (publickey)
fatal: Could not read from remote repository
```
### Solution :
- This happens because the iconland submodule uses an SSH URL by default, which requires GitHub SSH keys.
To make setup smoother for all contributors, I updated the README with troubleshooting steps to switch the submodule URL to HTTPS:

```
git submodule set-url iconland https://github.com/Newton-School/iconland.git
git submodule update --init --recursive
```


## Type of Change
- [x] 📚 Documentation update

issues.title: Bottom Navigation Bar Overlaps Content on Different Screen Sizes
issues.body: The bottom navigation bar is overlapping the login content on certain screen sizes and resolutions. This affects usability, as important actions (like buttons and links) become partially hidden or hard to interact with. ## The bottom navigation bar should: - Stay fixed without overlapping the main content, or - Push the content above it with proper spacing (padding/margin), or - Be responsive and reposition itself based on screen height.

issues.title: Feature Request: Invite Member to Group via Email (EmailJS Integration)
issues.body: ## Feature Request: Invite Member to Group via Email (EmailJS Integration) **📝 Description** Currently, the "Expense Splitter" application allows users to create groups and manage expenses. To make collaboration easier, we need a feature that allows group admins or current members to invite new friends to a group directly via email. This feature will utilize EmailJS to handle the delivery of invitation emails reliably. **🚀 User Story** _As a group member,_ I want to click an "Invite Member" button and enter a friend's email address, So that they receive an email invitation to join the specific group. **💻 Proposed Solution** 1. **UI Changes** - **"Invite Member" Button:** - Location: Add a button (e.g., a "User Plus" icon or "Invite" text) to the Group Cards on the Dashboard or inside the Group Details view. - Interaction: Clicking this button opens the Invitation Modal. - **Invitation Modal:** - Header: "Invite Friend to [Group Name]" - Input: Text field for Email Address (with validation feedback). - Actions: - Send Invite (Primary Button) - Cancel (Secondary Button) 2. **Logic & Functionality** - **Validation:** - Ensure the email field is not empty. - Validate standard email format (Regex). - **EmailJS Integration:** - Use the EmailJS SDK to send the email. - Service ID: `REACT_APP_EMAILJS_SERVICE_ID` - Template ID: `REACT_APP_EMAILJS_TEMPLATE_ID` - Public Key: `REACT_APP_EMAILJS_PUBLIC_KEY` - Template Parameters: Pass dynamic data to the template: `to_email`, `from_name` (current user), `group_name`, and `invite_url` (if applicable). 3. **User Feedback** - Loading State: Show a spinner or disable the "Send" button while the API request is in flight. - Success State: Close the modal and show a Toast notification: "Invitation sent successfully to [email]!" - Error State: Keep the modal open and display an error message if the email fails to send. **🧪 Verification & Testing** _To ensure the feature works finely:_ - **Positive Test:** Enter a valid email -> Click Send -> Verify toast appears -> Verify email arrives in inbox. - **Negative Test:** Enter "invalid-email" -> Click Send -> Verify validation error appears. - **Network Test:** Disconnect internet -> Click Send -> Verify graceful error message. Error State: Keep the modal open and display an error message if the email fails to send.

issues.title: Bug: Dashboard totals not showing correct amounts
issues.body: # 🐞 Bug: Dashboard totals not showing correct amounts ### 📄 Description On the **Dashboard** page of the Expense Splitter app, the summary cards **"You Owe"** and **"You Are Owed"** always display `$0.00`, even when expenses are present. While the **Recent Activity** section correctly shows expense entries with non-zero amounts, these values are not being reflected in the dashboard totals. ### 🔁 Steps to Reproduce 1. Log in to the application. 2. Create or join a group. 3. Add one or more expenses. 4. Navigate to the **Dashboard**. ### ✅ Expected Behavior - **You Owe** should show the total calculated amount the user owes others. - **You Are Owed** should show the total calculated amount others owe the user. - Totals should update automatically immediately after adding expenses. ### ❌ Actual Behavior - Both **You Owe** and **You Are Owed** persist at `$0.00`. - **Recent Activity** displays non-zero expense values correctly, indicating the data exists but isn't aggregating. ### 📸 Screenshot *(Place screenshot here showing $0.00 totals vs expenses in Recent Activity) or update late* ### 🔍 Possible Causes - **Calculation Logic:** Dashboard totals might not be aggregating the expense array correctly. - **State Management:** Frontend state might not be triggering a re-render or re-fetch for the totals after expense creation. - **Backend:** The API/DB query responsible for the summary endpoints might be failing to return aggregated values.

issues.title: Feature Request: Implement Google Authentication
Bug Report: Theme Switching Not Working
issues.body:
## 🐛 Description
The "Theme" setting in the Settings page (Light / Dark / Auto) is currently non-functional. Selecting a different theme updates the setting in the backend, but the UI does not change to reflect the selection.

## 🕵️ Analysis
Upon investigation, the following issues were identified:
1.  **Missing Tailwind Configuration**: `tailwind.config.js` is missing the `darkMode: 'class'` configuration, which is required for manual theme toggling.
2.  **No Theme Application Logic**: There is no code in the frontend (e.g., in `App.jsx` or a context provider) that listens to the theme setting and adds/removes the `dark` class from the `<html>` or `<body>` element.
3.  **State Isolation**: The theme state is currently isolated within `Settings.jsx` and is not shared globally, meaning other components don't know which theme is active.

## 🛠 Proposed Fix
To fix this, we need to implement the following:

### 1. Update Tailwind Config
Enable class-based dark mode in `tailwind.config.js`:
```javascript
export default {
  darkMode: 'class',
  // ... rest of config
}
```

### 2. Global Theme State
-   Add a `theme` property to the global store (`useStore.js`).
-   Initialize it from `localStorage` (for immediate load) or the user's settings.

### 3. Theme Applicator
-   Create a `useEffect` hook (likely in `App.jsx` or `Layout.jsx`) that runs whenever the `theme` state changes.
-   This hook should:
    -   Check if the theme is 'dark' (or 'auto' and the system is dark).
    -   Add or remove the `dark` class from `document.documentElement`.
    -   Persist the choice to `localStorage`.

### 4. Sync Settings
-   Ensure that changing the theme in `Settings.jsx` updates the global store, which in turn triggers the visual change.

## 📅 Acceptance Criteria
-   [ ] Selecting "Dark" immediately switches the UI to dark mode.
-   [ ] Selecting "Light" immediately switches the UI to light mode.
-   [ ] Selecting "Auto" respects the operating system's color scheme.
-   [ ] The selected theme persists after a page reload.

issues.title: Feature Request: Comprehensive Group Section Implementation
issues.body:
# Feature Request: Comprehensive Group Section Implementation

## 🚀 Overview
The current "Group Section" (Dashboard, Group Details, Manage Group) is functional but requires significant improvements to be production-ready. This issue tracks the work needed to "properly implement" the group management features, ensuring robustness, security, and a seamless user experience.

## 📋 Tasks

### 1. Group Creation (Dashboard)
- [ ] **Validation**: Ensure group name is not empty and has a reasonable length limit.
- [ ] **Error Handling**: Display user-friendly error messages if creation fails (e.g., server error).
- [ ] **UI Polish**: Add a loading state to the "Create Group" button to prevent double submissions.

### 2. Member Management (Manage Group)
- [ ] **Invite Flow**: Currently, adding a member creates a *new* user with a placeholder email. This needs to be changed to:
    -   Search for existing users by email.
    -   If found, add them to the group.
    -   If not found, send an email invitation (using the backend's `sendGroupInvitationEmail` endpoint).
- [ ] **Remove Member**: Ensure that removing a member also handles their outstanding balances (or prevents removal if they have debts).
- [ ] **Role Management**: (Optional) Add "Admin" vs "Member" roles to restrict who can delete the group or remove members.

### 3. Group Details & Expenses
- [ ] **Real-time Updates**: Implement polling or WebSockets to update the expense list when another user adds an expense.
- [ ] **Expense Validation**:
    -   Prevent adding expenses with 0 amount.
    -   Ensure split percentages add up to exactly 100%.
    -   Ensure split shares are valid numbers.
- [ ] **Settlements**:
    -   Verify that "Settle Up" correctly records the transaction and updates balances immediately.
    -   Add a confirmation dialog before recording a settlement.

### 4. General Improvements
- [ ] **Loading States**: Ensure all data fetching (groups, expenses, members) has proper skeleton loaders.
- [ ] **Error Boundaries**: Wrap components in Error Boundaries to prevent the entire app from crashing if one component fails.
- [ ] **Responsive Design**: Verify that all group-related pages look perfect on mobile devices.

## 🛠 Technical Details
- **Frontend**: React, Tailwind CSS, Zustand (Store)
- **Backend API**:
    -   `POST /groups` (Create)
    -   `POST /groups/:id/invite` (Invite Member)
    -   `DELETE /groups/:id/members/:userId` (Remove Member)
    -   `POST /expenses` (Add Expense)

## 📅 Acceptance Criteria
- Users can create groups with valid data.
- Users can invite *real* existing users to groups via email.
- Expenses and settlements are calculated accurately and updated in real-time (or near real-time).
- The UI handles loading and error states gracefully.

issues.title: [Bug] page navigation is broken after first chart load
issues.body:
## What is the current behavior?
On the official Recharts examples page (e.g., https://recharts.github.io/examples/), the sidebar navigation stops working after the "Run" button in the code editor is used.
-  ### Steps to Reproduce:
1. Navigate to any chart example on the official website, for instance: https://recharts.github.io/examples/SimpleAreaChart

2. In the code editor on the right, click the "Run" button.

3. The chart re-renders correctly, as expected.

4. Now, in the left-hand sidebar, click on any other chart link (e.g., "Tiny Bar Chart" or "Two Level Pie Chart").

Current Behavior: The page does not update. The content (title, chart, and code) remains stuck on the "Simple Area Chart" example. Subsequent clicks on other links also fail.

Screenshots or videos are welcome.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cd57d340-082a-4b16-b2e3-30fb3ee852a9

## What is the expected behavior?
After clicking the "Run" button on an example, the sidebar navigation should continue to function normally. Clicking a different chart link (e.g., "Tiny Bar Chart") should successfully load the new chart's example page, with its corresponding title, visualization, and code.

## Please provide a demo of the problem in a sandbox

Here is how: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/How-to-share-a-demo

Please try to keep the demo as small as possible while still showing the problem.

## Which versions of Recharts, and which browser / OS are affected by this issue? Did this work in previous versions of Recharts?

issues.title: Remove a login form student
issues.body:
## Description

Based on our app's user flow, the **student experience** should be immediate and not require any login. The login screen is intended **only for drivers**.

Currently, the main welcome screen (from the initial design) presents two options:
1.  "I'm a Student"
2.  "I'm a Driver"

This issue is to implement the correct navigation for the "I'm a Student" button.

## Expected Behavior

When a user taps the **"I'm a Student"** button on the welcome screen, they should be immediately navigated directly to the main **Home Page** (the screen that shows "Active Routes," etc.).

They should **not** see a login or signup page.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> add screenshot while raised a PR

issues.title: Toggle theme button (light and Dark)

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
Create a toggle button that allows users to switch between dark mode and light mode.
-   Test the toggle functionality across pages.
> you can use the ShadCN for the attractive buttons.

issues.title: Login page for the Student and Driver
issues.body:
# On the home page show two button ```Student```  and ``` Driver```
- On click of the respective button, the user should be redirected to their corresponding login page (e.g., student login, driver login).
## Create a login page which includes:
- Username field
- password field
- Login button


#### And you style using a Tailwind..
